content: The free version of Zulip only stores the most recent 10000 messages. Unfortunately the upgrade costs per member, which makes it far too expensive. So, the simplest solution is that we backup the data periodically, and store it in a shared drive/repository - preferably one which is easy to search.

Export Data: Exports all users, settings, and all data visible in public streams. Any organization administrator can conduct an export. Depending on the size of your organization, an export can take anywhere from seconds to an hour.

The only limit is 5 exports per week.
I think I'm going to try it now, and see how user-friendly the backup is.
An alterntive would be moving to a private server. I am sure some university could host it for us
yeah. here's the info: https://zulip.readthedocs.io/en/latest/production/export-and-import.html#backups

https://zulipchat.com/plans/
Zulip doesn't usually charge full price except to private organisations
it's free for open source projects
if you sent them an email, I'm sure they would either reduce the price steeply, or even make it free
it looks good for Zulip if their service is being used to host mathematicians and scientists for an entire field :)

yes, I'm only concerned about moving to a server because of this one line:

Backups aren’t useful for migrating organizations between self-hosting and Zulip Cloud (which may require renumbering all the users/messages/etc.).

but we'll ask them about it.
I should've thought about this stuff, but I really had no idea it would blow up so much.
yeah that's a good point. we'll ask them.
wait, where are you seeing the thing about only charging private organizations?
If backups are no use we should move this to a server asap.
I can ask Jelle if we can host it, if you want

Christian Williams said:

wait, where are you seeing the thing about only charging private organizations?

You can scroll down in the F.A.Q. section ;).
okay, it backed up within 10 seconds and downloaded a 40MB tar.gz
now let's see how to view it
yeah, to do this stuff right you need to already make your own server.
I guess I can just try it, for practice.
whoops, spent all that time installing and then it wouldn't accept my ucr email address. one more time...

Problem binding to port 80: Could not bind to IPv4 or IPv6.

I think I'm misunderstanding something in the installation process.
anyone else wanna try, or should I ask for help on the development server?
it's possible you're already using port 80 for something; it's a popular port
can you try using some other port number?
how so? it's not an installer option that I can see
ah, if it was on the command line, I would have thought you could pass something like --port 3001 to it
but perhaps not
yeah, it says unrecognized option
oh, you need port 80 so that it can be connected to via a browser normally
probably your port 80 isn't set up to allow incoming traffic — I imagine you'll need to set up port forwarding
the development server seems like a good place to ask, though

yeah, I tried to just stop apache to free up the port, but then I got

The following errors were reported by the server:

Domain: zulip.categorytheory.com
Type: unauthorized
Detail: Invalid response from
http://zulip.categorytheory.com/.well-known/acme-challenge/vyRUxots91ME0REtKm6X09bzS3DY523kqDTrG_LQCBc
[208.91.197.27]: "<!DOCTYPE HTML PUBLIC \"-//IETF//DTD HTML
2.0//EN\">\n<html><head>\n<title>404 Not
Found</title>\n</head><body>\n<h1>Not Found</h1>\n<p"

To fix these errors, please make sure that your domain name was
entered correctly and the DNS A/AAAA record(s) for that domain
contain(s) the right IP address.
I'm asking on chat.zulip.org
@Fabrizio Genovese did you ask Jelle if he would host for us?

Christian Williams said:

Fabrizio Genovese did you ask Jelle if he would host for us?

I did, awaiting reply
one of the main Zulip developers said that they will sponsor our server (give us the upgrade for free)!
WHOHOO
So nice of them :).
awesome!
yeah! honestly I really just made the mistake of not reading the https://zulipchat.com/plans/ page fully.
they only want to charge servers which are using it commercially (where the members are being paid by whoever owns it)
so any noncommercial organization can just email support@zulipchat.com and ask for the free Standard version.
to be fair, it's a surprisingly generous policy
yes, it is. I've been very happy with this platform so far
I believe that’s because they are still in market capturing phase so we could take advantage of that. Businesses always have a hidden motive of directly or indirectly investing revenue in future ;)
I've just looked at how much they charge commercial organisations, and now it seems reasonable that they can well afford to host open organisations if they just have a few big companies
which seems like a win for everyone

Nathanael Arkor said:

I've just looked at how much they charge commercial organisations, and now it seems reasonable that they can well afford to host open organisations if they just have a few big companies

I understand your point of view, but working in a company I can guarantee you that the overhead cost is big. Salaries to pay, infrastructure to mantain etc. Also we did like 40Mb of storage in what? Less than 3 days? You see that costs can pile up pretty quickly. So to me it's still remarkable that they made this offer. What they have to gain is clearly business image, it's always good when researchers use your platforms.
I am probably underestimating costs, you're right
now that I think about it, 40 MB sounds really high
at a very rough estimate, that's ~35k messages
@Christian Williams can probably come up with a number in the administration section.

https://categorytheory.zulipchat.com/stats
apparently ~2k messages have been sent
so I wonder why it takes up that much space; maybe quite a lot of it is constant overhead
I think it's also that you're probably misjudging the length of the messages. This message is 111 bytes long!
Add in some significant meta-information and it can definitely get out of hand.
Sure, backups are compressed, but still.
I was approximating each message as being 140 characters, UTF-8, but the metadata is probably quite significant
This bot API sure is interesting btw...
We should come up with bot-related functionality that we want to have!
(deleted)
